---
title: "Data Transfers"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Data Transfers}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

The *rdataset* package stores the data associated with your microbial DNA analysis. This tutorial will explain how to save, load, copy, export, and import your dataset object. If you haven't reviewed the [Getting Started](Getting-Started.html) tuturial, we recommend you start there. First let's load the rdataset package.

```{r setup}
library(rdataset)
```
We can use the **miseq_sop_example** function to create a dataset object from the [Miseq SOP Example](https://mothur.org/wiki/miseq_sop/). 

```{r}
miseq <- miseq_sop_example()
miseq
```

## Save

## Load

## Copy

## Export

## Import
